name: East India Relish
recipeIngredient: 1 quart Firmly packed shredded carrots (about 1 pound)
1 quart Peeled, diced green tomatoes (about 8 medium)
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir a few tablespoons of this relish into mayonnaise when making a chicken salad, or try it with pot roast.
2. The recipe makes six 1-pint jars. You'll need a shredder or food processor in addition to the basic equipment for boiling water bath canning.
3. Organize ingredients, equipment, and work space.
4. In a preserving kettle, combine all ingredients. Heat over medium-high heat until it reaches boiling point. Reduce the heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
5. Spoon the relish evenly into hot jars, leaving 1/4 inch from the top.
6. Pour hot liquid from the kettle into jars, leaving 1/4 inch from the top of each jar. Use a non-metallic tool to release any air bubbles along the inside of the jars. Add more relish if necessary to maintain 1/4 inch from the top.
7. Wipe the tops and threads of the jars with a damp clean cloth.
8. Place lids on the jars and screw bands according to the manufacturer's instructions.
9. Process the jars in a boiling water bath for 15 minutes. Follow the standard steps for boiling water bath canning, numbers 10 through 19.
